signals.title: USD/JPY — Fed Hawkish Shift + BoJ Hike Bias + Crowded Short
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.93 — Fed's hawkish shift (MUFG, ING) supports higher Dollar, while BoJ is hiking (BoJ 1.0% policy rate, TONA 0.73% OIS). This creates a +290bp carry premium favoring USD. JPY COT LF net short −115,036 (week ending Jun 9) is an extremely crowded speculative short position, diverging from the BoJ's hike bias. Options market shows elevated hedging demand with USD/JPY IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.4% (VRP +4.8%). Intervention risk remains high on a sustained break above 160.00, but carry and Fed hawkishness are currently dominating. A BoJ policy surprise or explicit intervention is required to unwind this crowded short.

signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + BoE Caution
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3232 — GBP is the weakest G10 currency this week, averaging −0.722% vs peers (n=4 pairs), while USD is the strongest at +0.951% (n=7 pairs), creating a significant 1.673% spread. This bilateral theme is reinforced by ING's view that BoE caution weighs on Sterling, with the BoE expected to keep rates steady at 3.75% (BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp carry neutral). Elevated options hedging demand is also present with GBP/USD IV 11.0% vs HV30 7.2% (VRP +3.8%). The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) is a key watch for any shift in forward guidance.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5763 — COT LF net short −22,354 (week ending Jun 9) indicates speculative positioning is heavily short NZD, diverging from the RBNZ's market bias for a hike. This creates short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ signals or delivers a hike at its next meeting. Retail sentiment is extremely long NZD (91% long), further highlighting a potential contrarian setup if institutional positioning unwinds. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 20% hike priced) on 8 Jul is the catalyst.

signals.title: CHF — SNB Intervention Stance + Accommodative Real Rate
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.8036 — SNB's Schlegel dodged questions on adding 'if necessary' to FX intervention readiness, suggesting a cautious but present stance. The CHF real policy rate is -0.60% (SNB 0.0% vs inflation expectations 0.60%), indicating a mildly accommodative monetary policy despite the SNB being on hold. This accommodative stance is likely to limit CHF's safe-haven appeal unless global risk-off sentiment intensifies significantly (VIX > 25). The next SNB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) determines the forward guidance.
